headline: Chinese Blockade Risks Put TSMC’s Central Chip Role Under Scrutiny
summary: Rising geopolitical tensions with China have sharpened focus on the risk of a potential blockade of Taiwan. Such a move could severely disrupt Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing’s chip production and shipments for key clients including Nvidia, AMD, Broadcom, and Apple. Investors are assessing how a prolonged disruption could affect global supply chains in technology, autos, and energy, while possibly shifting demand toward non Taiwanese chipmakers and shipping companies. Taiwan...

headline: Apple’s Gemini Siri Overhaul Puts AI Privacy And Valuation In Focus
summary: Apple, ticker NasdaqGS:AAPL, is preparing a major Siri overhaul for iOS 27 that uses new AI capabilities and tighter privacy controls. The update is set to integrate Google Gemini AI within Apple’s ecosystem while emphasizing user control and automatic data minimization. This move marks a shift in how Apple approaches AI partnerships, virtual assistants and on-device privacy. For Apple, the company behind the iPhone, iPad and Mac, this kind of AI refresh is about more than a new software...

headline: Epic Games' 'Fortnite' returns to App Stores worldwide
summary: Epic Games said on Tuesday its popular shooter title "Fortnite" has returned to global App ‌Stores, as the videogame maker signaled confidence in ‌a favorable outcome from an ongoing lawsuit against Apple.  "Once Apple is forced ​to show its costs, governments around the world will not allow Apple junk fees to stand," Epic said in a statement.  Epic, a U.S.-based studio backed by China's Tencent, has ‌been embroiled in a ⁠legal battle with Apple since 2020 and alleged that the company's practice of charging a ⁠commission of up to 30% on in-app payments violated U.S. antitrust rules.

content.title: Epic Games' 'Fortnite' returns to App Stores worldwide
content.summary: Epic Games said on Tuesday its popular shooter title "Fortnite" has returned to global App ‌Stores, as the videogame maker signaled confidence in ‌a favorable outcome from an ongoing lawsuit against Apple.  "Once Apple is forced ​to show its costs, governments around the world will not allow Apple junk fees to stand," Epic said in a statement.  Epic, a U.S.-based studio backed by China's Tencent, has ‌been embroiled in a ⁠legal battle with Apple since 2020 and alleged that the company's practice of charging a ⁠commission of up to 30% on in-app payments violated U.S. antitrust rules.
